title: Treacle tart
subtitle: Golden syrup and breadcrumb tart, baked in a shortcrust case
excerpt: A British tart filled with a mixture of golden syrup, fresh white breadcrumbs, and lemon juice. The filling sets to a soft, chewy, intensely sweet slab. Best served warm with cream. Makes one 23 cm tart.
sourceNotes: Florence White, Good Things in England (1932): treacle tart documented as an English household pudding made with golden syrup and breadcrumbs in a shortcrust case. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: golden syrup tarts are noted alongside jam tarts in the household pastry entries. The addition of fresh breadcrumbs and lemon juice to the syrup filling is the standard form recorded in British household baking manuals from the Victorian period.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The tart keeps for 3 days. Serve warm or at room temperature.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; 190°C in a conventional oven

glossaryTerms.definition: Baking a pastry case without its filling. The empty case is lined with baking paper, weighted with dried beans or ceramic baking beans, and baked until the pastry is set and pale gold. The beans hold the sides in place and prevent the base from puffing. The paper and beans are then removed for a further few minutes to dry and crisp the base before the filling is added.

content.text: Treacle tart is almost embarrassingly simple. The filling is three ingredients: golden syrup, breadcrumbs, and lemon. The breadcrumbs absorb the syrup and give the filling body without making it solid. Use fresh white breadcrumbs rather than dried; dried absorb less and the filling sets too firm.

items.prepNote: or fresh white breadcrumbs from day-old bread

content.text: Make the pastry and blind bake

Rub the cold {{unsalted-butter}} into the {{plain-flour}} and {{sea-salt-fine}} until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs. Add 2–3 tbsp cold water and press together to a smooth dough. Wrap and chill for 30 minutes. Preheat the oven to 180°C fan. Roll the pastry to 3 mm thick and line a 23 cm loose-bottomed tart tin.
for 15 minutes with baking paper and baking beans, then remove the beans and paper and bake for a further 5 minutes until pale gold.

Warm the {{golden-syrup}} gently until runny. Stir in the {{breadcrumbs-dried}}, {{lemon}} juice and zest, and {{double-cream}} if using. The filling should be thick enough to pour but not stiff. Pour into the pastry case and bake for 20–25 minutes, until the filling is set and just beginning to colour at the edges. The centre will still wobble slightly; it firms as it cools. Serve warm.
